name: Steamed Oysters with Black Beans
recipeIngredient: 2 teaspoons Fermented black beans, rinsed and drained
1 tablespoon Shao-hsing wine or dry sherry
2 tablespoons Chicken stock or low-sodium broth
1 small Scallion, green part only, thinly sliced
recipeInstructions:
1. Place the oysters on a large heatproof dish. Divide the black beans evenly and place atop each oyster.
2. In a small bowl, combine the soy sauce, wine, sugar, vinegar, chicken stock, sesame oil, peanut oil, and white pepper. Distribute the mixture over each oyster.
3. Bring 6 cups of water to a boil over high heat in a wok with a steamer rack or in a steamer pot. Place the dish on the steamer rack and steam the oysters until plumped and opaque, about 2-3 minutes. Do not overcook; the oysters will be tough if cooked longer.
4. Sprinkle the red pepper and scallion on top.
5. Place 2 oysters on each of 6 serving plates. Serve hot.
6 First-Course Servings. 69 Calories and 157 Mg Sodium per serving.
